steps.title: Assign quantities to each pan size
steps.content:
From the problem:
  • 9-inch pans: $n$ (the defined variable)
  • 10-inch pans: $4n$ (four times the number of 9-inch pans)
  • 7-inch pans: $10$ (given directly)

steps.title: Write the total equation
steps.content:
All three quantities must sum to 100:
$$4n + n + 10 = 100$$
Combine like terms:
$$5n + 10 = 100$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $10\left(4n\right)+9n+7\left(10\right)=100$ — This equation multiplies the pan quantities by the pan sizes (10, 9, 7 inches), which gives total inches rather than total count. The problem asks for a count equation. Eliminated.
  • B: $10n+9n+7n=100$ — This treats every pan size as $n$ pans, ignoring that 7-inch pans $= 10$ and 10-inch pans $= 4n$. Eliminated.
  • C: $4n+10=100$ — This omits the 9-inch pans ($n$). Eliminated.
  • D: $5n+10=100$ — This correctly combines $4n + n = 5n$ (10-inch plus 9-inch pans) and adds the 10 fixed 7-inch pans. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
